title: Indulgent Crunchy Malaysian Kueh Cake
description: A sweet and crunchy Malaysian dessert featuring a jammy center and a crunchy exterior, made with sugar-free golden syrup and emulsified using Manteca.
instructions: Line a 20cm (8-inch) round cake pan with parchment paper.
Mix flour, baking powder, and salt in a medium bowl.
In a large bowl, cream together Manteca and unsalted butter until light and fluffy.
Add sugar-free golden syrup and mix until well combined.
Beat in egg yolks one at a time.
Gradually add flour mixture, then mix in condensed milk and palm sugar syrup.
Pour batter into prepared cake pan and smooth top.
Bake for 35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Remove from oven and let cool in pan for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.
Before serving, top with a mixture of crushed nuts, desiccated coconut, and a drizzle of palm sugar syrup.
